sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, dt TIMESTAMP, val INT);
INSERT INTO test_dates (dt, val) VALUES (date '2020-01-01', 1), (date '2020-12-31', 2), (date '2021-03-01', 3), (NULL, 4);
SELECT week(dt), val FROM test_dates WHERE dt IS NOT NULL AND week(dt) BETWEEN 1 AND 53;
SELECT dt + (interval '1' second * (7 * 24 * 60 * 60)) AS new_date, week(dt + (interval '1' second * (7 * 24 * 60 * 60))) AS new_week FROM test_dates;
SELECT dt, week(dt), val FROM test_dates t1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_dates t2 WHERE t1.val < t2.val);
SELECT dt, COUNT(*) AS count_val FROM test_dates GROUP BY week(dt) HAVING week(dt) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT dt FROM test_dates ORDER BY week(dt) DESC, dt + (interval '1' day) LIMIT 5;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT dt, week(dt) AS week_num FROM test_dates WHERE val IS NOT NULL) AS subquery WHERE week_num = (SELECT MAX(week(dt)) FROM test_dates WHERE dt IS NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE test_nulls (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, dt TIMESTAMP NULL);
INSERT INTO test_nulls (dt) VALUES (NULL), (date '2020-08-15');
SELECT week(dt) AS iso_week FROM test_nulls GROUP BY iso_week HAVING iso_week IS NOT NULL;
SELECT dt + (interval '1' second * (1 + 2)) AS incremented_date FROM test_nulls;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates WHERE week(dt) IS NULL OR week(dt) BETWEEN 1 AND 53;
CREATE TABLE temp_dates (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, dt TIMESTAMPTZ, val INT);
INSERT INTO temp_dates (dt, val) VALUES (date '2019-01-01' AT TIME ZONE 'UTC', 10), (NULL, 20);
SELECT dt, week(dt) AS week_num FROM temp_dates WHERE dt IS NOT NULL AND week(dt) = week(NOW());
SELECT val FROM test_dates WHERE dt + (interval '1' day) <= current_timestamp GROUP BY val HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
SELECT dt, week(dt) FROM (SELECT dt FROM test_dates UNION SELECT dt FROM temp_dates) AS combined WHERE week(dt) IS NOT NULL;
ALTER TABLE test_dates ADD COLUMN additional_dt TIMESTAMP;
UPDATE test_dates SET additional_dt = dt + (interval '1' week) WHERE val = 1;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS test_nulls;
